ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: NIMH Global Obsessive-Compulsive Scale - Circle the number that best describes the present clinical state of the patient
Notes: 1 = Minimal within range of normal or very mild symptoms (Person spends little time resisting them. Almost no or no interference in daily activity) - low; 2 = Minimal within range of normal or very mild symptoms - medium; 3 = Minimal within range of normal or very mild symptoms - high; 4 = Subclinical obsessive-compulsive behavior (Mild symptoms that are noticeable to patient and observer, cause mild interference in patient's life and which he may resist for a minimal period of time. Easily tolerated by others) - low; 5 = Subclinical obsessive-compulsive behavior - medium; 6 = Subclinical obsessive-compulsive behavior - high; 7 = Clinical obsessive-compulsive behavior (Symptoms that cause significant interference in patient's life and which he spends a great deal of conscious energy resisting. Requires some help from others to function in daily activity) - low; 8 = Clinical obsessive-compulsive behavior - medium; 9 = Clinical obsessive-compulsive behavior - high; 10 = Severe obsessive-compulsive behavior (Symptoms that are crippling to the patient, interfering so that daily activity is ''an active struggle.'' Patient may spend full time resisting symptoms. Requires much help from others to function) - low; 11 = Severe obsessive-compulsive behavior - medium; 12 = Severe obsessive-compulsive behavior - high; 13 = Very severe obsessive-compulsive behavior (Symptoms that completely cripple patient so that he requires close staff supervision over eating, sleeping, and so forth. Very minor decision making or minimal activity require staff support, ''worst I've ever seen'') - low; 14 = Very severe obsessive-compulsive behavior - medium; 15 = Very severe obsessive-compulsive behavior - high
e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily
